jobs.title: Magnet Program Director
jobs.description:
Come lead with us at Houston Methodist Sugar Land Hospital

At Houston Methodist, the Magnet Program Director (MPD) position is responsible for providing leadership, planning and coordination in the Magnet application process, preparation, designation, maintenance and related databases to drive business goals, cultivating a professional practice environment. This position is accountable for leading change efforts in collaboration with nurse leaders and staff utilizing the ANCC Magnet framework of excellence to create and implement innovative staff engagement, systems and processes. The MPD position identifies cutting edge nursing quality indicators, developmental needs, performance improvement, and research initiatives and other activities that sustain and advance a culture of excellence and serves as an ambassador of the Magnet program employing a variety of communication strategies. This position collaborates with Houston Methodist system hospitals for sharing of best practices, reporting methodologies and identification of potential duplication of effort.

FLSA STATUS

Exempt

QUALIFICATIONS

EDUCATION

Bachelor’s and Master’s degree, one which must be from an accredited school of nursing

EXPERIENCE

Five years of experience in healthcare
Three years prior experience with project/program management in a Magnet health care setting preferred
High-level knowledge and experience with executing strategic programs to drive business success
Knowledge of Magnet standards and experience in a Magnet organization preferred

LICENSES AND CERTIFICATIONS

Required

RN - Registered Nurse - Texas State Licensure - Texas Board of Nursing_PSV Compact Licensure – Must obtain permanent Texas license within 60 days (if establishing Texas residency) and
Magnet-ANCC Recognized Certification (HM)

SKILLS AND ABILITIES

Demonstrates the skills and competencies necessary to safely perform the assigned job, determined through ongoing skills, competency assessments, and performance evaluations
Sufficient proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing the English language necessary to perform the essential functions of this job, especially with regard to activities impacting patient or employee safety or security
Ability to effectively communicate with patients, physicians, family members and co-workers in a manner consistent with a customer service focus and application of positive language principles
Demonstrates outstanding relationship skills to partner with key stakeholders to drive program success
Maintains expertise in Magnet standards and active in identification of exemplars and collection of evidence, application development, writing and submission of all required documents
Strong organizational/project management and presentation skills
Excellent critical synthesis and problem-solving skills
Knowledgeable of adult learning principles
Well versed in computer skills of the entire Microsoft Office Suite (Access, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word)

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

PEOPLE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Develops and implements communication strategies throughout the organization/system to ensure accurate information regarding program purpose, initiatives, improvement strategies, and outcomes. Manages dissemination of information around Magnet initiatives via e-mail, newsletter articles, hardcopy handbook and presentations.
Supports nursing structural empowerment through education, mentorship, consultation and team building.

SERVICE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Plans, directs, and executes all Magnet initiatives within documented timeframe(s) to ensure the goals of the program are met which includes overseeing and coordinating the Magnet application, document submission, site visit and/or re-designation processes.
Serves as a knowledge expert and resource in interpreting and integrating the Magnet standards, implementing and sustaining shared governance environment at the unit and hospital-wide level to ensure that the organization milieu reflects the internalization of the five components of the Magnet Model.
Provides consultative services as needed, which includes serving as Magnet advisor to nursing leadership and staff to drive program to successful outcomes. Supports development and adoption of standards for evidence-based knowledge practice. Facilitates integration of Magnet recommendations into nursing practice and outcome achievement.
Develops materials, presentations, and reports on program for Senior Leadership, Management, and front-line staff, providing education, and developing strategies to respond to new program requirements and opportunities for improvement supported by external benchmarking.

QUALITY/SAFETY ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Collaborates with key stakeholders to customize and manage and monitor changes to databases responsive to nursing's needs, e.g., National Database for Nursing Quality Indicators (NDNQI), Magnet Recognition Program reports, demographic reports, and RN engagement surveys. Analyzes, synthesizes, and trends multi-year nurse engagement survey reports and supports action plans with recommendations for hospital-wide initiatives.
Functions as liaison to the ANCC Magnet Recognition Program office, employing principles of continuous performance improvement to advance the profession of nursing. Reviews changes related to the Magnet Recognition Program and facilitates integration within the hospital.
Collaborates to ensure collection, correlation, analysis and generation of detailed reports and integrated quality data to Senior Leadership and other key stakeholders regarding Magnet Recognition Program.

FINANCE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Manages the Magnet program within documented timeframes and within budget. Drives efficiencies and quality improvements, recognizing current and future resource limitations and needs.

GROWTH/INNOVATION ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Implements change management strategies to ensure successful execution of Magnet program. Develops, coordinates and evaluates programs to advance a culture of excellence, including special events, ongoing Magnet campaign and various communication strategies.
Advances Houston Methodist’s external image to the public in the Magnet field through external meeting attendance, publication and speaking engagements. Participates in hospital-wide and system committees as a designee of the Chief Nursing Officer/Executive.
Provides and supports strategic direction and oversight for Magnet program and uses appropriate and evolving methodologies to drive program initiatives.
Assumes an active role in promoting professional and other work environment initiatives, e.g., engages and mentors nurses to advance publications and presentations.
Conducts literature searches and other benchmarking inquiries that substantiate evidence-based and best practices and supports research initiatives, including the design and implementation of data collection systems.

SUPPLEMENTAL REQUIREMENTS

WORK ATTIRE

Uniform: No
Scrubs: Yes
Business professional: Yes
Other (department approved): No

ON-CALL*

Note that employees may be required to be on-call during emergencies (ie. Disaster, Severe Weather Events, etc) regardless of selection below.
On Call* No

TRAVEL**

Travel specifications may vary by department**
May require travel within the Houston Metropolitan area Yes
May require travel outside Houston Metropolitan area Yes

EXPERIENCE

Five years of experience in healthcare
Three years prior experience with project/program management in a Magnet health care setting preferred
High-level knowledge and experience with executing strategic programs to drive business success
Knowledge of Magnet standards and experience in a Magnet organization preferred

LICENSES AND CERTIFICATIONS

Required

RN - Registered Nurse - Texas State Licensure - Texas Board of Nursing_PSV Compact Licensure – Must obtain permanent Texas license within 60 days (if establishing Texas residency) and
Magnet-ANCC Recognized Certification (HM)

Houston Methodist is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Hospitals and Health Care

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Company Description
Golden Sands Capital partners with businesses from initial application through securing investment, helping them become pitch-ready and set for sustained growth. The firm provides access to a curated network of investors who offer both funding and mentorship to support dynamic expansion. Beyond capital, Golden Sands Capital delivers comprehensive support, including investor-ready documentation, expert legal counsel, and strategic guidance. Its network also connects clients with wealth managers, tax advisors, and strategic consultants, offering the expertise needed to scale on a solid, long-term foundation.
Role Description
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) is a full-time, remote role responsible for setting and executing the overall strategic direction of Golden Sands Capital. The CEO will oversee day-to-day operations, ensuring effective delivery of investment support services, coordination with legal and advisory partners, and alignment of internal processes with organizational goals. This role includes leading executive decision-making on business planning, financial performance, and resource allocation, as well as monitoring key metrics and implementing improvements. The CEO will represent the organization in high-level investor and client meetings, cultivate strategic partnerships, and promote a culture of innovation, accountability, and inclusive leadership. The position requires close collaboration with senior leaders to drive growth, maintain strong stakeholder relationships, and ensure compliance with relevant regulations and industry standards.
Qualifications

Strong financial acumen with proven experience in Finance
and investment-related decision-making.
Demonstrated expertise in Operations Management
, including leading remote teams and optimizing systems and processes.
Proficiency in Business Planning
, including forecasting, budgeting, and developing scalable business models.
Track record in Sales
and business development, with the ability to build and maintain high-value stakeholder relationships.
Advanced Strategic Planning
skills, with experience defining vision, setting priorities, and executing long-term growth strategies.
Prior executive leadership experience in finance, investment, or professional services environments.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to represent the organization publicly.
Comfort operating in a remote, digital-first setting, with strong decision-making and problem-solving abilities.
Relevant advanced degree (e.g., MBA, finance, economics, or related field) is preferred but not required.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Company Description
Creditus Financial Company is a financial services organization headquartered on Av. Getúlio Vargas in Jacarezinho, Paraná, Brazil. The company focuses on providing financial solutions and services tailored to the needs of individuals and businesses. As a growing player in the financial sector, Creditus aims to strengthen its market presence through innovation, responsible practices, and customer-centric offerings. Team members collaborate across functions to drive sustainable growth and deliver value to clients. The organization encourages initiative, accountability, and continuous improvement in all aspects of its operations.
Role Description
This is a full-time remote role for a Chief Executive Officer (CEO). The CEO will provide overall strategic leadership, define long-term business objectives, and ensure alignment of financial and operational goals. Responsibilities include overseeing company-wide operations, guiding business planning processes, setting performance targets, and monitoring key metrics. The CEO will lead and support sales initiatives, develop strategic partnerships, and represent the organization in external engagements. This role also involves building and mentoring a high-performing leadership team, fostering an inclusive culture, and ensuring compliance with relevant financial regulations and corporate governance standards.
Qualifications

Strong Finance and financial management skills, including budgeting, forecasting, and understanding of financial statements.
Proficiency in Operations Management and Business Planning, with experience optimizing processes and driving organizational efficiency.
Demonstrated capability in Sales and revenue growth, including setting sales strategies and supporting client relationship development.
Expertise in Strategic Planning, with a track record of defining and executing long-term business strategies.
Proven executive leadership experience in financial services or a related industry, preferably in a senior management or C-level role.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and decision-making skills, with the ability to lead diverse, remote teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with comfort working with data-driven insights and performance metrics.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field; an advanced degree (e.g., MBA) is an asset.
High level of integrity, commitment to ethical business practices, and familiarity with regulatory and compliance requirements in financial services.

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Industries

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Company Description
DecryptoX is a decentralized exchange platform focused on empowering users with advanced trading tools and comprehensive DeFi solutions. The platform emphasizes a privacy-first approach by integrating decentralized identity (DID) technologies, providing secure and user-centric trading experiences. DecryptoX aims to make digital asset trading more accessible, transparent, and efficient for a global user base. The organization is committed to innovation in decentralized finance and to building products that align with the evolving needs of crypto traders and investors.
Role Description
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) intern at DecryptoX will support the overall strategic and operational direction of the platform in collaboration with the founding team. Day-to-day responsibilities include contributing to business planning, assisting with financial and operational analysis, and helping to coordinate cross-functional initiatives across product, marketing, and technology. The CEO intern will participate in strategic discussions related to positioning in the DeFi and DID ecosystem, support the development of growth strategies, and provide input on sales and partnership opportunities. This is an internship role and is fully remote, requiring strong self-management, effective communication, and the ability to collaborate across time zones.
Qualifications

Strong foundation in Finance and financial analysis, with the ability to interpret data and support decision-making.
Experience or coursework in Operations Management and Business Planning, including process optimization and resource coordination.
Skills in Sales and relationship-building, with interest in identifying partnership and growth opportunities.
Strategic Planning capabilities, including market analysis, competitive assessment, and long-term roadmap support.
Interest in or exposure to blockchain, DeFi, and decentralized identity (DID) technologies.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information clearly.
Ability to work independently in a remote environment, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines.
Pursuing or holding a degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Technology Management, or a related field is highly beneficial.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Internship

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Role Overview:

The CEO is the top executive responsible for setting the strategic direction, building and leading the leadership team, ensuring sustainable financial performance, and delivering on the company’s mission and long-term value for stakeholders. The CEO partners with the board to establish strategy, culture, and governance, and leads cross-functional execution across all functions.
Key Responsibilities

Strategy and Leadership
Define and articulate the company’s vision, strategy, and long-term roadmap.
Translate strategy into actionable goals, OKRs, and operating plans.
Lead, motivate, and develop a high-performing executive leadership team.
Governance and Stakeholder Management
Partner with the board of directors to align on strategy, risk, and performance.
Ensure effective governance, transparency, and regulatory compliance.
Cultivate relationships with key stakeholders (investors, customers, partners, regulators, community).

Financial Stewardship

Own and monitor financial performance: revenue growth, profitability, cash flow, capital allocation.
Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and long-range financial planning.
Lead fundraising, investor relations, and capital strategy as applicable.
Operations and Execution
Optimize organizational structure, processes, and culture to enable scalable execution.
Drive product/service quality, customer experience, and operational efficiency.
Implement data-driven decision-making and a culture of accountability.

Talent and Culture

Attract, retain, and develop top leadership and critical capabilities.
Foster an inclusive, high-performance culture aligned with core values.
Steward succession planning and leadership development.
Risk Management and Compliance
Identify, assess, and mitigate strategic, financial, regulatory, and operational risks.
Ensure robust internal controls, cybersecurity, and data governance where relevant.

External Representation

Serve as the chief spokesperson and brand ambassador.
Lead strategic partnerships, alliances, and market expansion efforts.
Innovation and Market Position
Lead investments in R&D, product innovation, and go-to-market strategy.
Monitor competitive landscape and adjust strategy to sustain differentiation.
Performance and Accountability
Define, monitor, and report on KPIs aligned with strategy and stakeholder expectations.
Establish a cadence of reviews with the board and leadership team.
Risk Management and Compliance
Identify, assess, and mitigate strategic, financial, regulatory, and operational risks. Ensure robust internal controls, cybersecurity, and data governance where relevant.
External Representation Serve as the chief spokesperson and brand ambassador. Lead strategic partnerships, alliances, and market expansion efforts. Innovation and Market Position Lead investments in R&D, product innovation, and go-to-market strategy. Monitor competitive landscape and adjust strategy to sustain differentiation.
Performance and Accountability Define, monitor, and report on KPIs aligned with strategy and stakeholder expectations. Establish a cadence of reviews with the board and leadership team.

Qualifications and Experience

Education: Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred. Relevant professional certifications may be considered.
Experience: 15+ years in senior leadership roles; proven track record of [revenue growth, profitability, scaling businesses, market expansion, fundraising] in [industry/sector].
Leadership: Demonstrated experience building and leading diverse, high-performing teams; strong change management capabilities.
Financial Acumen: Deep understanding of P&L, balance sheets, cash flow management, and capital markets.

Core Competencies

Strategic Thinking and Vision
People Leadership and Culture
Financial Stewardship
Change Management and Execution
Customer and Market Orientation
Negotiation and Partnership Building Integrity, Ethics, and Governance
Results-Driven and Data-Informed Decision Making
Growth-stage: scale operations, expand markets, optimize unit economics, strengthen governance. Mature/public: investor relations, compliance, strategic portfolio management, and sustained value creation.

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diverse perspectives. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.
Responsibilities

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Role Description
The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a full-time remote role responsible for overseeing day-to-day operations and driving organizational performance across all business functions. The COO will lead operational planning, align processes with corporate goals, and ensure efficient order fulfillment, supplier management, and customer service practices. This role will work closely with executive leadership to develop and execute business strategies, optimize workflows, and monitor key performance indicators for both private and government sales channels. The COO will manage budgeting and financial oversight related to operations, ensure compliance with internal standards and external requirements, and support the integration of new product lines and supplier relationships. The person in this role will also mentor operational teams, promote continuous improvement, and help maintain a culture focused on reliability, quality, and service.
Qualifications

Strong Business Planning skills, including strategic planning and the ability to translate corporate goals into operational roadmaps.
Proven Operations Management experience, with a track record of optimizing processes, managing teams, and improving efficiency.
Advanced Analytical Skills for interpreting data, monitoring KPIs, and making evidence-based decisions.
Solid Finance and Budgeting skills, with experience managing operational budgets and contributing to financial forecasting.
Experience in industrial supply, distribution, or a related sector is highly beneficial.
Demonstrated leadership, communication, and stakeholder management abilities in a remote or distributed work environment.
Familiarity with government contracting, bid processes, and compliance standards is an asset.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Operations Management, Finance, or a related field; advanced degree or relevant certifications are a plus.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Chief Operating Officer (COO) — Electrical Equipment Manufacturing

Position Type:
Full-Time, Executive
Reports To:
Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
Company Overview

We are a fast-growing manufacturer in the electrical power industry, specializing in medium- and low-voltage power distribution and electrical equipment — including switchboards, transformers, switchgear, mobile substations, e-houses, and energy-as-a-service solutions. We serve utility, industrial, data center, and renewable energy markets across North America. Our business is organized as a set of focused product divisions backed by a single, shared manufacturing and delivery organization. As we scale our operations and expand our footprint in Florida, we are seeking a seasoned operations executive to lead and consolidate our enterprise delivery engine.
Position Summary

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is the single executive accountable for everything between a customer order and a satisfied customer. Reporting to the CEO, the COO provides strategic leadership and day-to-day oversight of the company’s entire delivery engine — manufacturing, supply chain, quality, project management, and field service — delivered as one shared organization that serves every product division.
The COO partners closely with the division General Managers, who own commercial strategy and divisional P&L, while the COO owns how well the company builds, ships, and stands behind its products. The ideal candidate is a hands-on operations leader with deep roots in electrical equipment or heavy industrial manufacturing who can convert rising demand into delivered, profitable revenue — on time, at quality, and at target margin — across multiple divisions and plants.
Key Responsibilities

Operational Execution & Leadership

Direct and oversee all manufacturing and delivery operations across divisions, ensuring products ship on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.
Translate company strategy into operational plans, setting clear KPIs for production, safety, quality, and efficiency.
Foster a culture of accountability, safety, and continuous improvement across the factory floor, project teams, and functional organizations.
Own cross-division coordination, resolving competing priorities between divisions and shared functions through clear service-level agreements.
Partner with the CEO and Board of Directors to define long-term capital investment strategy and capacity planning.

Manufacturing & Supply Chain

Own manufacturing strategy, capacity planning, and unit-cost performance across all plants.
Direct supply chain strategy, supplier partnerships, purchasing, and inventory to ensure material availability and mitigate market volatility for critical raw materials (e.g., copper, silicon steel, insulation).
Drive throughput, yield, and On-Time-In-Full (OTIF) delivery performance.

Quality, Compliance & Standards

Establish and own a robust Quality Management System (QMS) compliant with ISO and industry-specific standards (UL, ANSI/IEEE, IEC, and DFARS where applicable).
Drive defect reduction, first-pass yield, and audit performance across the product portfolio.

Project Execution & Field Service

Own PMO discipline and the on-time, on-budget execution of projects and customer orders.
Lead field-service execution and aftermarket performance to ensure strong customer outcomes after delivery.

Process Improvement (Lean & Six Sigma)

Lead the implementation of Lean Manufacturing principles to reduce waste, optimize inventory, and streamline workflows.
Utilize Six Sigma methodologies to drive defect reduction and process-variability improvements.
Institutionalize documented SOPs and a single operating cadence so process discipline is durable, not personality-dependent.

Financial & Resource Management

Manage the P&L for operations, including budgeting, forecasting, and cost-control initiatives.
Analyze operational data to identify bottlenecks and opportunities for margin expansion.
Lead ERP and CRM (Salesforce) adoption and maintain a transparent enterprise operational KPI dashboard.
Mentor and develop a high-performing leadership team across manufacturing, supply chain, quality, PMO, and field service.

Qualifications & Requirements

Experience:

Minimum of 12 years
of progressive operations leadership in electrical equipment, power distribution, or heavy industrial manufacturing, including senior responsibility for multi-plant, multi-product operations.
Proven track record of managing manufacturing, supply chain, quality, and engineering teams in an engineered-to-order environment.
Demonstrated success building and scaling operations through significant revenue growth.
Methodologies:

Demonstrated expertise in Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma
(Black Belt certification is a plus).
Strong history of implementing continuous-improvement programs that resulted in measurable cost savings or efficiency gains.
Hands-on experience implementing ERP and CRM (Salesforce) discipline, SOPs, and a structured KPI cadence.
Education:

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, or Industrial) or Business Administration required.
Master’s degree (MBA or MS in Engineering) is highly preferred.

Core Competencies

Deep operational understanding of electrical equipment manufacturing, testing, and delivery.
Strong financial acumen and data-driven decision-making skills.
Excellent executive communication, negotiation, and cross-functional leadership abilities.
Comfort holding single-point accountability and running a transparent KPI cadence.
Crisis management and problem-solving under pressure.

Compensation & Benefits

Competitive Executive Base Salary.
Performance-based Annual Bonus.
Comprehensive Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance.
401(k) with Company Match.
Relocation Package.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

General Business

Industries

Appliances, Electrical, and Electronics Manufacturing, Electrical Equipment Manufacturing, and Electric Power Transmission, Control, and Distribution

jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
Chief Development Officer

Location: Remote (Eastern U.S. preferred) with travel

About the Company

Our client is a leading national provider of home-based healthcare services backed by one of the top middle-market private equity firms in the country. The organization delivers a broad range of in-home care services, including skilled home health, hospice, behavioral health, and personal care, serving tens of thousands of patients across multiple states.

With a mission centered on delivering high-quality, compassionate care in the home, the company partners closely with physicians, health systems, and payors to improve outcomes and reduce the total cost of care. The organization has experienced significant growth through a combination of organic expansion and strategic acquisitions and continues to invest in initiatives that strengthen its national footprint and leadership in home-based care.

Position Overview

The Chief Development Officer will serve as a key member of the executive leadership team and lead the organization’s enterprise growth and expansion strategy. Reporting directly to the CEO, this executive will oversee all corporate development activities, including mergers and acquisitions, market expansion, strategic partnerships, and de novo growth initiatives.

This is a highly strategic and operational leadership role designed for an experienced healthcare executive who can build and scale a sophisticated development function within a rapidly growing, private equity-backed organization. The Chief Development Officer will partner closely with executive leadership, operational leaders, and private equity sponsors to identify and execute opportunities that accelerate growth, expand market presence, and enhance enterprise value.

The ideal candidate will bring deep healthcare M&A expertise, strong strategic and financial acumen, and a proven ability to lead complex growth initiatives in dynamic, high-growth environments.

Key Responsibilities

Enterprise Growth & Strategic Leadership

Partner closely with the CEO, executive leadership team, and private equity stakeholders to shape and execute the company’s long-term growth vision.

Drive enterprise-wide expansion initiatives, including acquisitions, market entry strategies, and de novo growth opportunities across targeted regions and service lines.

Analyze industry trends, competitive landscapes, reimbursement dynamics, and emerging market opportunities to guide strategic decision-making.

Serve as a trusted advisor to senior leadership on growth strategy, scalability, investment opportunities, and organizational transformation initiatives.

Mergers & Acquisitions

Lead the full lifecycle of M&A activities, including deal sourcing, valuation, due diligence, negotiation, structuring, and transaction execution.

Build and enhance a high-performing corporate development function, including operational processes, infrastructure, reporting capabilities, and team development.

Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with founders, advisors, investment bankers, private equity firms, and key industry partners.

Manage and prioritize the acquisition pipeline while ensuring disciplined assessment of strategic and financial opportunities.

Collaborate cross-functionally with finance, legal, operations, clinical, and integration teams throughout the transaction process.

Expansion & Integration

Spearhead strategic expansion initiatives across both existing and emerging markets.

Evaluate and execute de novo growth opportunities in partnership with operational leadership teams.

Oversee integration planning and post-acquisition value creation efforts to ensure successful business alignment and performance optimization.

Partner with operational and financial leaders to track acquisition outcomes and implement continuous improvement initiatives.

Establish scalable frameworks, governance models, and best practices to support sustainable long-term growth.

Core Competencies

Strategic, data-driven, and execution-focused leadership approach.

Strong financial, operational, and commercial acumen.

Demonstrated success leading enterprise growth initiatives within complex healthcare organizations.

Entrepreneurial mindset with experience building teams, infrastructure, and scalable processes in high-growth environments.

Exceptional communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.

Executive presence with the ability to influence leadership teams, investors, and external partners.

Collaborative and team-oriented leadership style with a low-ego, hands-on approach.

Ability to thrive in fast-paced, evolving environments while managing multiple priorities effectively.

Qualifications

12+ years of experience in healthcare corporate development, investment banking, private equity, or executive leadership roles focused on growth strategy and M&A.

Deep healthcare services transaction experience required. Experience in home health, hospice, behavioral health, or post-acute care strongly preferred.

Proven track record leading complex acquisitions and growth initiatives within private equity-backed or rapidly scaling organizations.

Strong expertise in transaction execution, valuation, financial modeling, and strategic analysis.

Experience building or scaling a corporate development or M&A function preferred.

Demonstrated ability to work effectively with executive leadership teams, boards, and private equity sponsors.

MBA or other advanced degree preferred.

Why Join Us

Opportunity to lead the growth strategy of a rapidly expanding national healthcare organization.

Direct collaboration with the CEO and private equity sponsors on transformative, high-impact initiatives.

Ability to build and shape a sophisticated corporate development and M&A platform from the ground up.

Executive leadership role with broad organizational influence and visibility.

Entrepreneurial and growth-focused environment with substantial long-term leadership opportunities.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development, Health Care Provider, and Strategy/Planning

Industries

Hospitals and Health Care

jobs.title: DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS
jobs.description:
Save this job

Email

Tweet

Facebook

LinkedIn

Description

JOB OVERVIEW
:

Provide guidance and leadership to the Rooms and the Engineering Division, ensuring consistent compliance with hotel policies, and quality guest service while maximizing departmental profits.

Reports To

General Manager

Essential Job Functions

Implement and manage hotel’s daily quality process including goal communication, associate improvement, compliance with Crescent standards of product and performance, service recovery and problem resolution. Disseminate feedback from comment cards, guest satisfaction and service failure measurements and coach accordingly.

Interview, hire, train, recommend performance evaluations, resolve problems, provide open communication and recommend discipline and/or termination when appropriate.

Communicate both verbally and in writing to provide clear direction to staff.

Assign and instruct Rooms and the Engineering Division Department Managers in details of work. Observe performance and encourage improvement. Monitor hotel occupancy and make staffing adjustments accordingly. Supervise and review cost and inventory controls.

Field guest complaints, conducting thorough research to develop the most effective solutions and negotiate results. Prepare written correspondence to guests. Listen and extend assistance in order to resolve problems such as price conflicts, insufficient heating or air conditioning, etc. Remain calm and alert especially during emergency situations and heavy hotel activity. Plan and implement detailed steps or problem resolution by using experienced judgment and discretion.

Supervise the budgeting, forecasting, training, motivating and staffing of the Rooms and Engineering Department including: Telephone, Housekeeping Services, Front Office, Laundry, Concierge and Guest Services and Engineering.

Prepare Forecast expenses and actual results for the Rooms and the Engineering Division revenue and expenses. Review Security logs daily for significant incidents, and coordinate with department heads all enforcement of policy and/or improvements in service needed.

Work closely with the General Manager in establishing and monitoring policies and guidelines in the day to day operation of the hotel to ensure profitability and consistency. Solve problems of detail that come up in the course of the work.

Plan, organize, chair, attend and/or participate in various hotel meetings such as: Staff Meetings, Rooms and Engineering Division Meetings, Executive Committee Meetings, Quality Teams Meetings, etc.

Comply with attendance rules and be available to work on a regular basis.

Perform any other job related duties as assigned.

Required Skills And Abilities

Must have the ability to communicate in English. Self-starting personality with an even disposition. Maintain a professional appearance and manner at all times. Can communicate well with guests. Must be willing to “pitch-in” and help co- workers with their job duties and be a team player. Requires the exercise of considerable managerial skill as position involves frequent decisions, meeting of deadlines, negotiations of contracts of vendors, analytical ability and the planning, organization development and coordination of large scale work projects. Ability to effectively deal with internal and external customers, some of whom will require high levels of patience, tact and diplomacy to defuse anger and collect accurate information and resolve conflicts. Complex mathematical skills and considerable skill in the use of a calculator to prepare complex mathematical calculations without error, i.e. budgets, forecasting. Ability to be mobile for significant distances between and within buildings on the property. Ability to observe performance and detect signs of emergency situations and respond with proper action.

PERFORMANCE STANDARDS

Customer Satisfaction

Our customers are what we are about. One of the keys to a positive guest experience is positive interaction with Crescent staff. It is essential that you remain professional at all times, and that you treat all guests and associates with courtesy and respect, under all circumstances. Every Crescent associate is a guest relations ambassador, every working minute of every day.

Work Habits

In order to maintain a positive guest and associate experience, your work habits should always meet and strive to exceed hotel standards for work procedures, dress, grooming, punctuality and attendance. You should be adaptable to change in your work area and in hotel procedures with a willingness to learn new skills and/or improve existing ones, have the ability to solve routine problems that occur on the job and ask for help whenever you are not sure how to do something.

Safety & Security

The safety and security of our guests and associates is of utmost importance to Crescent. Every Crescent associate should adhere to the hotel security policies and procedures, particularly regarding key controls, lifting heavy objects, using chemicals, and effectively reporting safety hazards and safety concerns.

Note

This description excludes non-essential and marginal functions of the position that are incidental to the performance of the fundamental job duties. Furthermore, the specific examples in each section are not intended to be all-inclusive. Rather, they represent the typical elements and criteria considered necessary to perform the job successfully. Other job-related duties may be assigned by the associate’s supervisor.

Furthermore, this description is subject to change, in the sole discretion of the Company, and in no way creates an employment contract, implied or otherwise; each associate remains, at all times, an “at will” associate.

High school diploma required.

For applicants who will be working in California, the anticipated compensation range for the position is $ 100,000.00 - 120,000.00 per year. The salary offered to a successful candidate will be dependent on several factors that may include, but are not limited to years of experience within the job, years of experience within the required industry, education, work location, etc. Crescent Hotels is a multi-state employer and the salary ranges indicated herein may not reflect positions that work only in other states.

new_jobs.title: Magnet Program Director
new_jobs.description:
Come lead with us at Houston Methodist Sugar Land Hospital

At Houston Methodist, the Magnet Program Director (MPD) position is responsible for providing leadership, planning and coordination in the Magnet application process, preparation, designation, maintenance and related databases to drive business goals, cultivating a professional practice environment. This position is accountable for leading change efforts in collaboration with nurse leaders and staff utilizing the ANCC Magnet framework of excellence to create and implement innovative staff engagement, systems and processes. The MPD position identifies cutting edge nursing quality indicators, developmental needs, performance improvement, and research initiatives and other activities that sustain and advance a culture of excellence and serves as an ambassador of the Magnet program employing a variety of communication strategies. This position collaborates with Houston Methodist system hospitals for sharing of best practices, reporting methodologies and identification of potential duplication of effort.

FLSA STATUS

Exempt

QUALIFICATIONS

EDUCATION

Bachelor’s and Master’s degree, one which must be from an accredited school of nursing

EXPERIENCE

Five years of experience in healthcare
Three years prior experience with project/program management in a Magnet health care setting preferred
High-level knowledge and experience with executing strategic programs to drive business success
Knowledge of Magnet standards and experience in a Magnet organization preferred

LICENSES AND CERTIFICATIONS

Required

RN - Registered Nurse - Texas State Licensure - Texas Board of Nursing_PSV Compact Licensure – Must obtain permanent Texas license within 60 days (if establishing Texas residency) and
Magnet-ANCC Recognized Certification (HM)

SKILLS AND ABILITIES

Demonstrates the skills and competencies necessary to safely perform the assigned job, determined through ongoing skills, competency assessments, and performance evaluations
Sufficient proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing the English language necessary to perform the essential functions of this job, especially with regard to activities impacting patient or employee safety or security
Ability to effectively communicate with patients, physicians, family members and co-workers in a manner consistent with a customer service focus and application of positive language principles
Demonstrates outstanding relationship skills to partner with key stakeholders to drive program success
Maintains expertise in Magnet standards and active in identification of exemplars and collection of evidence, application development, writing and submission of all required documents
Strong organizational/project management and presentation skills
Excellent critical synthesis and problem-solving skills
Knowledgeable of adult learning principles
Well versed in computer skills of the entire Microsoft Office Suite (Access, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word)

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

PEOPLE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Develops and implements communication strategies throughout the organization/system to ensure accurate information regarding program purpose, initiatives, improvement strategies, and outcomes. Manages dissemination of information around Magnet initiatives via e-mail, newsletter articles, hardcopy handbook and presentations.
Supports nursing structural empowerment through education, mentorship, consultation and team building.

SERVICE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Plans, directs, and executes all Magnet initiatives within documented timeframe(s) to ensure the goals of the program are met which includes overseeing and coordinating the Magnet application, document submission, site visit and/or re-designation processes.
Serves as a knowledge expert and resource in interpreting and integrating the Magnet standards, implementing and sustaining shared governance environment at the unit and hospital-wide level to ensure that the organization milieu reflects the internalization of the five components of the Magnet Model.
Provides consultative services as needed, which includes serving as Magnet advisor to nursing leadership and staff to drive program to successful outcomes. Supports development and adoption of standards for evidence-based knowledge practice. Facilitates integration of Magnet recommendations into nursing practice and outcome achievement.
Develops materials, presentations, and reports on program for Senior Leadership, Management, and front-line staff, providing education, and developing strategies to respond to new program requirements and opportunities for improvement supported by external benchmarking.

QUALITY/SAFETY ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Collaborates with key stakeholders to customize and manage and monitor changes to databases responsive to nursing's needs, e.g., National Database for Nursing Quality Indicators (NDNQI), Magnet Recognition Program reports, demographic reports, and RN engagement surveys. Analyzes, synthesizes, and trends multi-year nurse engagement survey reports and supports action plans with recommendations for hospital-wide initiatives.
Functions as liaison to the ANCC Magnet Recognition Program office, employing principles of continuous performance improvement to advance the profession of nursing. Reviews changes related to the Magnet Recognition Program and facilitates integration within the hospital.
Collaborates to ensure collection, correlation, analysis and generation of detailed reports and integrated quality data to Senior Leadership and other key stakeholders regarding Magnet Recognition Program.

FINANCE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Manages the Magnet program within documented timeframes and within budget. Drives efficiencies and quality improvements, recognizing current and future resource limitations and needs.

GROWTH/INNOVATION ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Implements change management strategies to ensure successful execution of Magnet program. Develops, coordinates and evaluates programs to advance a culture of excellence, including special events, ongoing Magnet campaign and various communication strategies.
Advances Houston Methodist’s external image to the public in the Magnet field through external meeting attendance, publication and speaking engagements. Participates in hospital-wide and system committees as a designee of the Chief Nursing Officer/Executive.
Provides and supports strategic direction and oversight for Magnet program and uses appropriate and evolving methodologies to drive program initiatives.
Assumes an active role in promoting professional and other work environment initiatives, e.g., engages and mentors nurses to advance publications and presentations.
Conducts literature searches and other benchmarking inquiries that substantiate evidence-based and best practices and supports research initiatives, including the design and implementation of data collection systems.

SUPPLEMENTAL REQUIREMENTS

WORK ATTIRE

Uniform: No
Scrubs: Yes
Business professional: Yes
Other (department approved): No

ON-CALL*

Note that employees may be required to be on-call during emergencies (ie. Disaster, Severe Weather Events, etc) regardless of selection below.
On Call* No

TRAVEL**

Travel specifications may vary by department**
May require travel within the Houston Metropolitan area Yes
May require travel outside Houston Metropolitan area Yes

EXPERIENCE

Five years of experience in healthcare
Three years prior experience with project/program management in a Magnet health care setting preferred
High-level knowledge and experience with executing strategic programs to drive business success
Knowledge of Magnet standards and experience in a Magnet organization preferred

LICENSES AND CERTIFICATIONS

Required

RN - Registered Nurse - Texas State Licensure - Texas Board of Nursing_PSV Compact Licensure – Must obtain permanent Texas license within 60 days (if establishing Texas residency) and
Magnet-ANCC Recognized Certification (HM)

Houston Methodist is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Company Description
Golden Sands Capital partners with businesses from initial application through securing investment, helping them become pitch-ready and set for sustained growth. The firm provides access to a curated network of investors who offer both funding and mentorship to support dynamic expansion. Beyond capital, Golden Sands Capital delivers comprehensive support, including investor-ready documentation, expert legal counsel, and strategic guidance. Its network also connects clients with wealth managers, tax advisors, and strategic consultants, offering the expertise needed to scale on a solid, long-term foundation.
Role Description
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) is a full-time, remote role responsible for setting and executing the overall strategic direction of Golden Sands Capital. The CEO will oversee day-to-day operations, ensuring effective delivery of investment support services, coordination with legal and advisory partners, and alignment of internal processes with organizational goals. This role includes leading executive decision-making on business planning, financial performance, and resource allocation, as well as monitoring key metrics and implementing improvements. The CEO will represent the organization in high-level investor and client meetings, cultivate strategic partnerships, and promote a culture of innovation, accountability, and inclusive leadership. The position requires close collaboration with senior leaders to drive growth, maintain strong stakeholder relationships, and ensure compliance with relevant regulations and industry standards.
Qualifications

Strong financial acumen with proven experience in Finance
and investment-related decision-making.
Demonstrated expertise in Operations Management
, including leading remote teams and optimizing systems and processes.
Proficiency in Business Planning
, including forecasting, budgeting, and developing scalable business models.
Track record in Sales
and business development, with the ability to build and maintain high-value stakeholder relationships.
Advanced Strategic Planning
skills, with experience defining vision, setting priorities, and executing long-term growth strategies.
Prior executive leadership experience in finance, investment, or professional services environments.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to represent the organization publicly.
Comfort operating in a remote, digital-first setting, with strong decision-making and problem-solving abilities.
Relevant advanced degree (e.g., MBA, finance, economics, or related field) is preferred but not required.

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Company Description
Creditus Financial Company is a financial services organization headquartered on Av. Getúlio Vargas in Jacarezinho, Paraná, Brazil. The company focuses on providing financial solutions and services tailored to the needs of individuals and businesses. As a growing player in the financial sector, Creditus aims to strengthen its market presence through innovation, responsible practices, and customer-centric offerings. Team members collaborate across functions to drive sustainable growth and deliver value to clients. The organization encourages initiative, accountability, and continuous improvement in all aspects of its operations.
Role Description
This is a full-time remote role for a Chief Executive Officer (CEO). The CEO will provide overall strategic leadership, define long-term business objectives, and ensure alignment of financial and operational goals. Responsibilities include overseeing company-wide operations, guiding business planning processes, setting performance targets, and monitoring key metrics. The CEO will lead and support sales initiatives, develop strategic partnerships, and represent the organization in external engagements. This role also involves building and mentoring a high-performing leadership team, fostering an inclusive culture, and ensuring compliance with relevant financial regulations and corporate governance standards.
Qualifications

Strong Finance and financial management skills, including budgeting, forecasting, and understanding of financial statements.
Proficiency in Operations Management and Business Planning, with experience optimizing processes and driving organizational efficiency.
Demonstrated capability in Sales and revenue growth, including setting sales strategies and supporting client relationship development.
Expertise in Strategic Planning, with a track record of defining and executing long-term business strategies.
Proven executive leadership experience in financial services or a related industry, preferably in a senior management or C-level role.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and decision-making skills, with the ability to lead diverse, remote teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with comfort working with data-driven insights and performance metrics.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field; an advanced degree (e.g., MBA) is an asset.
High level of integrity, commitment to ethical business practices, and familiarity with regulatory and compliance requirements in financial services.

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Company Description
DecryptoX is a decentralized exchange platform focused on empowering users with advanced trading tools and comprehensive DeFi solutions. The platform emphasizes a privacy-first approach by integrating decentralized identity (DID) technologies, providing secure and user-centric trading experiences. DecryptoX aims to make digital asset trading more accessible, transparent, and efficient for a global user base. The organization is committed to innovation in decentralized finance and to building products that align with the evolving needs of crypto traders and investors.
Role Description
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) intern at DecryptoX will support the overall strategic and operational direction of the platform in collaboration with the founding team. Day-to-day responsibilities include contributing to business planning, assisting with financial and operational analysis, and helping to coordinate cross-functional initiatives across product, marketing, and technology. The CEO intern will participate in strategic discussions related to positioning in the DeFi and DID ecosystem, support the development of growth strategies, and provide input on sales and partnership opportunities. This is an internship role and is fully remote, requiring strong self-management, effective communication, and the ability to collaborate across time zones.
Qualifications

Strong foundation in Finance and financial analysis, with the ability to interpret data and support decision-making.
Experience or coursework in Operations Management and Business Planning, including process optimization and resource coordination.
Skills in Sales and relationship-building, with interest in identifying partnership and growth opportunities.
Strategic Planning capabilities, including market analysis, competitive assessment, and long-term roadmap support.
Interest in or exposure to blockchain, DeFi, and decentralized identity (DID) technologies.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information clearly.
Ability to work independently in a remote environment, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines.
Pursuing or holding a degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Technology Management, or a related field is highly beneficial.

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Role Overview:

The CEO is the top executive responsible for setting the strategic direction, building and leading the leadership team, ensuring sustainable financial performance, and delivering on the company’s mission and long-term value for stakeholders. The CEO partners with the board to establish strategy, culture, and governance, and leads cross-functional execution across all functions.
Key Responsibilities

Strategy and Leadership
Define and articulate the company’s vision, strategy, and long-term roadmap.
Translate strategy into actionable goals, OKRs, and operating plans.
Lead, motivate, and develop a high-performing executive leadership team.
Governance and Stakeholder Management
Partner with the board of directors to align on strategy, risk, and performance.
Ensure effective governance, transparency, and regulatory compliance.
Cultivate relationships with key stakeholders (investors, customers, partners, regulators, community).

Financial Stewardship

Own and monitor financial performance: revenue growth, profitability, cash flow, capital allocation.
Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and long-range financial planning.
Lead fundraising, investor relations, and capital strategy as applicable.
Operations and Execution
Optimize organizational structure, processes, and culture to enable scalable execution.
Drive product/service quality, customer experience, and operational efficiency.
Implement data-driven decision-making and a culture of accountability.

Talent and Culture

Attract, retain, and develop top leadership and critical capabilities.
Foster an inclusive, high-performance culture aligned with core values.
Steward succession planning and leadership development.
Risk Management and Compliance
Identify, assess, and mitigate strategic, financial, regulatory, and operational risks.
Ensure robust internal controls, cybersecurity, and data governance where relevant.

External Representation

Serve as the chief spokesperson and brand ambassador.
Lead strategic partnerships, alliances, and market expansion efforts.
Innovation and Market Position
Lead investments in R&D, product innovation, and go-to-market strategy.
Monitor competitive landscape and adjust strategy to sustain differentiation.
Performance and Accountability
Define, monitor, and report on KPIs aligned with strategy and stakeholder expectations.
Establish a cadence of reviews with the board and leadership team.
Risk Management and Compliance
Identify, assess, and mitigate strategic, financial, regulatory, and operational risks. Ensure robust internal controls, cybersecurity, and data governance where relevant.
External Representation Serve as the chief spokesperson and brand ambassador. Lead strategic partnerships, alliances, and market expansion efforts. Innovation and Market Position Lead investments in R&D, product innovation, and go-to-market strategy. Monitor competitive landscape and adjust strategy to sustain differentiation.
Performance and Accountability Define, monitor, and report on KPIs aligned with strategy and stakeholder expectations. Establish a cadence of reviews with the board and leadership team.

Qualifications and Experience

Education: Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred. Relevant professional certifications may be considered.
Experience: 15+ years in senior leadership roles; proven track record of [revenue growth, profitability, scaling businesses, market expansion, fundraising] in [industry/sector].
Leadership: Demonstrated experience building and leading diverse, high-performing teams; strong change management capabilities.
Financial Acumen: Deep understanding of P&L, balance sheets, cash flow management, and capital markets.

Core Competencies

Strategic Thinking and Vision
People Leadership and Culture
Financial Stewardship
Change Management and Execution
Customer and Market Orientation
Negotiation and Partnership Building Integrity, Ethics, and Governance
Results-Driven and Data-Informed Decision Making
Growth-stage: scale operations, expand markets, optimize unit economics, strengthen governance. Mature/public: investor relations, compliance, strategic portfolio management, and sustained value creation.

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diverse perspectives. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

new_jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
new_jobs.description:
Role Description
The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a full-time remote role responsible for overseeing day-to-day operations and driving organizational performance across all business functions. The COO will lead operational planning, align processes with corporate goals, and ensure efficient order fulfillment, supplier management, and customer service practices. This role will work closely with executive leadership to develop and execute business strategies, optimize workflows, and monitor key performance indicators for both private and government sales channels. The COO will manage budgeting and financial oversight related to operations, ensure compliance with internal standards and external requirements, and support the integration of new product lines and supplier relationships. The person in this role will also mentor operational teams, promote continuous improvement, and help maintain a culture focused on reliability, quality, and service.
Qualifications

Strong Business Planning skills, including strategic planning and the ability to translate corporate goals into operational roadmaps.
Proven Operations Management experience, with a track record of optimizing processes, managing teams, and improving efficiency.
Advanced Analytical Skills for interpreting data, monitoring KPIs, and making evidence-based decisions.
Solid Finance and Budgeting skills, with experience managing operational budgets and contributing to financial forecasting.
Experience in industrial supply, distribution, or a related sector is highly beneficial.
Demonstrated leadership, communication, and stakeholder management abilities in a remote or distributed work environment.
Familiarity with government contracting, bid processes, and compliance standards is an asset.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Operations Management, Finance, or a related field; advanced degree or relevant certifications are a plus.

new_jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
new_jobs.description:
Chief Operating Officer (COO) — Electrical Equipment Manufacturing

Position Type:
Full-Time, Executive
Reports To:
Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
Company Overview

We are a fast-growing manufacturer in the electrical power industry, specializing in medium- and low-voltage power distribution and electrical equipment — including switchboards, transformers, switchgear, mobile substations, e-houses, and energy-as-a-service solutions. We serve utility, industrial, data center, and renewable energy markets across North America. Our business is organized as a set of focused product divisions backed by a single, shared manufacturing and delivery organization. As we scale our operations and expand our footprint in Florida, we are seeking a seasoned operations executive to lead and consolidate our enterprise delivery engine.
Position Summary

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is the single executive accountable for everything between a customer order and a satisfied customer. Reporting to the CEO, the COO provides strategic leadership and day-to-day oversight of the company’s entire delivery engine — manufacturing, supply chain, quality, project management, and field service — delivered as one shared organization that serves every product division.
The COO partners closely with the division General Managers, who own commercial strategy and divisional P&L, while the COO owns how well the company builds, ships, and stands behind its products. The ideal candidate is a hands-on operations leader with deep roots in electrical equipment or heavy industrial manufacturing who can convert rising demand into delivered, profitable revenue — on time, at quality, and at target margin — across multiple divisions and plants.
Key Responsibilities

Operational Execution & Leadership

Direct and oversee all manufacturing and delivery operations across divisions, ensuring products ship on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.
Translate company strategy into operational plans, setting clear KPIs for production, safety, quality, and efficiency.
Foster a culture of accountability, safety, and continuous improvement across the factory floor, project teams, and functional organizations.
Own cross-division coordination, resolving competing priorities between divisions and shared functions through clear service-level agreements.
Partner with the CEO and Board of Directors to define long-term capital investment strategy and capacity planning.

Manufacturing & Supply Chain

Own manufacturing strategy, capacity planning, and unit-cost performance across all plants.
Direct supply chain strategy, supplier partnerships, purchasing, and inventory to ensure material availability and mitigate market volatility for critical raw materials (e.g., copper, silicon steel, insulation).
Drive throughput, yield, and On-Time-In-Full (OTIF) delivery performance.

Quality, Compliance & Standards

Establish and own a robust Quality Management System (QMS) compliant with ISO and industry-specific standards (UL, ANSI/IEEE, IEC, and DFARS where applicable).
Drive defect reduction, first-pass yield, and audit performance across the product portfolio.

Project Execution & Field Service

Own PMO discipline and the on-time, on-budget execution of projects and customer orders.
Lead field-service execution and aftermarket performance to ensure strong customer outcomes after delivery.

Process Improvement (Lean & Six Sigma)

Lead the implementation of Lean Manufacturing principles to reduce waste, optimize inventory, and streamline workflows.
Utilize Six Sigma methodologies to drive defect reduction and process-variability improvements.
Institutionalize documented SOPs and a single operating cadence so process discipline is durable, not personality-dependent.

Financial & Resource Management

Manage the P&L for operations, including budgeting, forecasting, and cost-control initiatives.
Analyze operational data to identify bottlenecks and opportunities for margin expansion.
Lead ERP and CRM (Salesforce) adoption and maintain a transparent enterprise operational KPI dashboard.
Mentor and develop a high-performing leadership team across manufacturing, supply chain, quality, PMO, and field service.

Qualifications & Requirements

Experience:

Minimum of 12 years
of progressive operations leadership in electrical equipment, power distribution, or heavy industrial manufacturing, including senior responsibility for multi-plant, multi-product operations.
Proven track record of managing manufacturing, supply chain, quality, and engineering teams in an engineered-to-order environment.
Demonstrated success building and scaling operations through significant revenue growth.
Methodologies:

Demonstrated expertise in Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma
(Black Belt certification is a plus).
Strong history of implementing continuous-improvement programs that resulted in measurable cost savings or efficiency gains.
Hands-on experience implementing ERP and CRM (Salesforce) discipline, SOPs, and a structured KPI cadence.
Education:

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, or Industrial) or Business Administration required.
Master’s degree (MBA or MS in Engineering) is highly preferred.

Core Competencies

Deep operational understanding of electrical equipment manufacturing, testing, and delivery.
Strong financial acumen and data-driven decision-making skills.
Excellent executive communication, negotiation, and cross-functional leadership abilities.
Comfort holding single-point accountability and running a transparent KPI cadence.
Crisis management and problem-solving under pressure.

Compensation & Benefits

Competitive Executive Base Salary.
Performance-based Annual Bonus.
Comprehensive Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance.
401(k) with Company Match.
Relocation Package.

Show less

Executive

new_jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
new_jobs.description:
Chief Development Officer

Location: Remote (Eastern U.S. preferred) with travel

About the Company

Our client is a leading national provider of home-based healthcare services backed by one of the top middle-market private equity firms in the country. The organization delivers a broad range of in-home care services, including skilled home health, hospice, behavioral health, and personal care, serving tens of thousands of patients across multiple states.

With a mission centered on delivering high-quality, compassionate care in the home, the company partners closely with physicians, health systems, and payors to improve outcomes and reduce the total cost of care. The organization has experienced significant growth through a combination of organic expansion and strategic acquisitions and continues to invest in initiatives that strengthen its national footprint and leadership in home-based care.

Position Overview

The Chief Development Officer will serve as a key member of the executive leadership team and lead the organization’s enterprise growth and expansion strategy. Reporting directly to the CEO, this executive will oversee all corporate development activities, including mergers and acquisitions, market expansion, strategic partnerships, and de novo growth initiatives.

This is a highly strategic and operational leadership role designed for an experienced healthcare executive who can build and scale a sophisticated development function within a rapidly growing, private equity-backed organization. The Chief Development Officer will partner closely with executive leadership, operational leaders, and private equity sponsors to identify and execute opportunities that accelerate growth, expand market presence, and enhance enterprise value.

The ideal candidate will bring deep healthcare M&A expertise, strong strategic and financial acumen, and a proven ability to lead complex growth initiatives in dynamic, high-growth environments.

Key Responsibilities

Enterprise Growth & Strategic Leadership

Partner closely with the CEO, executive leadership team, and private equity stakeholders to shape and execute the company’s long-term growth vision.

Drive enterprise-wide expansion initiatives, including acquisitions, market entry strategies, and de novo growth opportunities across targeted regions and service lines.

Analyze industry trends, competitive landscapes, reimbursement dynamics, and emerging market opportunities to guide strategic decision-making.

Serve as a trusted advisor to senior leadership on growth strategy, scalability, investment opportunities, and organizational transformation initiatives.

Mergers & Acquisitions

Lead the full lifecycle of M&A activities, including deal sourcing, valuation, due diligence, negotiation, structuring, and transaction execution.

Build and enhance a high-performing corporate development function, including operational processes, infrastructure, reporting capabilities, and team development.

Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with founders, advisors, investment bankers, private equity firms, and key industry partners.

Manage and prioritize the acquisition pipeline while ensuring disciplined assessment of strategic and financial opportunities.

Collaborate cross-functionally with finance, legal, operations, clinical, and integration teams throughout the transaction process.

Expansion & Integration

Spearhead strategic expansion initiatives across both existing and emerging markets.

Evaluate and execute de novo growth opportunities in partnership with operational leadership teams.

Oversee integration planning and post-acquisition value creation efforts to ensure successful business alignment and performance optimization.

Partner with operational and financial leaders to track acquisition outcomes and implement continuous improvement initiatives.

Establish scalable frameworks, governance models, and best practices to support sustainable long-term growth.

Core Competencies

Strategic, data-driven, and execution-focused leadership approach.

Strong financial, operational, and commercial acumen.

Demonstrated success leading enterprise growth initiatives within complex healthcare organizations.

Entrepreneurial mindset with experience building teams, infrastructure, and scalable processes in high-growth environments.

Exceptional communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.

Executive presence with the ability to influence leadership teams, investors, and external partners.

Collaborative and team-oriented leadership style with a low-ego, hands-on approach.

Ability to thrive in fast-paced, evolving environments while managing multiple priorities effectively.

Qualifications

12+ years of experience in healthcare corporate development, investment banking, private equity, or executive leadership roles focused on growth strategy and M&A.

Deep healthcare services transaction experience required. Experience in home health, hospice, behavioral health, or post-acute care strongly preferred.

Proven track record leading complex acquisitions and growth initiatives within private equity-backed or rapidly scaling organizations.

Strong expertise in transaction execution, valuation, financial modeling, and strategic analysis.

Experience building or scaling a corporate development or M&A function preferred.

Demonstrated ability to work effectively with executive leadership teams, boards, and private equity sponsors.

MBA or other advanced degree preferred.

Why Join Us

Opportunity to lead the growth strategy of a rapidly expanding national healthcare organization.

Direct collaboration with the CEO and private equity sponsors on transformative, high-impact initiatives.

Ability to build and shape a sophisticated corporate development and M&A platform from the ground up.

Executive leadership role with broad organizational influence and visibility.

Entrepreneurial and growth-focused environment with substantial long-term leadership opportunities.

Show more

Show less
